arabic: يُعْتَبَرُ التَّوْحِيدُ جَوْهَرَ الرِّسَالَةِ الْإِسْلَامِيَّةِ، وَهُوَ الْغَايَةُ الَّتِي خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِنْ أَجْلِهَا الْخَلْقَ لِيُفْرِدُوهُ بِالْعِبَادَةِ. وَقَدْ رَوَى مُعَاذُ بْنُ جَبَلٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: «حَقُّ اللَّهِ عَلَى الْعِبَادِ أَنْ يَعْبُدُوهُ وَلَا يُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئًا» [رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي كِتَابِ التَّوْحِيدِ]. وَيُشِيرُ شَيْخُ الْإِسْلَامِ ابْنُ تَيْمِيَّةَ إِلَى أَنَّ الْعِبَادَةَ الْحَقِيقِيَّةَ تَتَضَمَّنُ غَايَةَ الذُّلِّ لِلَّهِ مَعَ غَايَةِ الْمَحَبَّةِ لَهُ سُبْحَانَهُ. فَلَا يَكْفِي أَنْ يَعْتَرِفَ الْإِنْسَانُ بِوُجُودِ الْخَالِقِ فَقَطْ، بَلْ لَا بُدَّ مِنْ إِخْلَاصِ الدُّعَاءِ وَالتَّوَكُّلِ لَهُ وَحْدَهُ دُونَ سِوَاهُ. إِنَّ تَحْقِيقَ التَّوْحِيدِ يُطَهِّرُ النَّفْسَ مِنَ التَّعَلُّقِ بِالْمَخْلُوقِينَ وَيَمْنَحُ الْقَلْبَ طُمَأْنِينَةً لَا مَثِيلَ لَهَا. وَبِهَذَا يَتَحَرَّرُ الْإِنْسَانُ مِنْ عُبُودِيَّةِ الْهَوَى إِلَى عُبُودِيَّةِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ.
translation: Tevhid se smatra suštinom islamske poruke i on je cilj radi kojeg je Allah stvorio stvorenja — da bi Mu samo Njemu ibadet činili. Muaz ibn Džebel, radijallahu anhu, prenosi da je Vjerovjesnik, sallallahu alejhi ve sellem, rekao: 'Allahovo pravo kod robova je da Mu robuju i da Mu ništa ne pridružuju' [Bilježi Buhari u Knjizi o tevshidu]. Šejhul-islam Ibn Tejmijje ukazuje na to da istinski ibadet podrazumijeva vrhunac poniznosti pred Allahom uz vrhunac ljubavi prema Njemu, Slavljen neka je On. Nije dovoljno da čovjek samo prizna postojanje Stvoritelja, već je neophodno iskreno upućivati dovu i oslanjati se samo na Njega, mimo bilo koga drugog. Ostvarenje tevhida čisti dušu od vezanosti za stvorenja i daruje srcu neusporediv smiraj. Time se čovjek oslobađa robovanja strastima i prelazi u robovanje Gospodaru svjetova.
translationEn: Tawhid (monotheism) is considered the core of the Islamic message, and it is the purpose for which Allah created creation—to single Him out for worship. Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated that the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) said: 'The right of Allah over His servants is that they should worship Him and not associate anything with Him' [Narrated by al-Bukhari in the Book of Tawhid]. Sheikh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah points out that true worship includes the utmost humility before Allah along with the utmost love for Him, Glorified be He. It is not enough for a person to merely acknowledge the existence of the Creator; rather, one must be sincere in supplication and reliance on Him alone, without any others. Realizing Tawhid purifies the soul from attachment to created beings and grants the heart an unparalleled tranquility. In this way, a human is liberated from the slavery of whims to the servitude of the Lord of the worlds.
